title: thymelaea virgata subsp. broussonetii
intro: Thymelaea virgata subsp. broussonetii, also known as the 'spiny thyme tree' or 'bitter thyme', is a subspecies of the Thymelaea virgata plant, which is a type of shrub or small tree native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its bitter and astringent qualities, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Thymelaea virgata subsp. broussonetii are thought to include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of certain diseases. In traditional herbal medicine, Thymelaea virgata subsp. broussonetii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and may also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
